## [00:00 - 03:02]

Okay, so now I want to talk about the artist's path through Product Launch Formula. Now, I have to admit when I first came out with Product Launch Formula, I didn't expect a lot of artists to use it. But lo and behold, they bought the product, they used it, and it worked for them. We're talking about sculptors and novelists and recording artists and painters. And it's just absolutely awesome because I love art. I've got a real artistic streak myself, and it was just awesome. So I started getting these success stories in, and it really makes sense because if you look at the way Hollywood does it, when they release a film, which is an artistic work, they totally use it. They totally sequence it out. They have the trailers, and they find all different ways to get it in popular culture, and they create buzz around their launch. So it absolutely works. So Product Launch Formula sequencing, awesome for art, whatever kind of artist you are. But it's not so much the PS path, not that problem solution path. Those mental triggers still work. The sequencing still works, but you're not really defining a problem and then solving that problem. I mean, you are delivering a lot of value. I mean, really, if you want to think about their problem, is they need entertainment, you're giving them a solution, yeah, sure, but it's not a perfect fit into that problem solution path. But you still want to tell a story throughout your pre-launch content. It's really the story of the creation of the artwork, whether it's a novel or a painting or whatever. You want to foreshadow the work. So that's one of the easiest, biggest things you want to do. I mean, it's like a movie trailer. What's that? It's a massive foreshadowing of the work. So this is why you always want to be building your list so that you can use that list to foreshadow the work, whether it's social media or email. I highly recommend email, but social media is for most artists is also an important adjunct to your email list. Remember that Hero's Journey and the product launch Hero's Journey, well, here you're telling, it's sort of the artist's Hero's Journey. You tell the story of the creation. If you think about it, like the making of, when they come out with the making of the Titanic or the making of this, the making of that, it's always a really interesting, it's the behind the scenes. People love that to feel like they're there, behind the scenes in there. They got an insider view. They love that. So if you do that leading up into the launch, that is absolutely awesome. And there's this other, another piece is a little bit of Revelation of Yourself. So if you're an artist, people, they instantly assume that you have a super romantic life. There's something really cool going on. People love that. I know I've gotten to meet some famous authors and it's just being able to rub shoulders

## [03:02 - 05:08]

with them is pretty amazing. And the thing is, you might think, oh, well, I'm just sitting at my desk here typing away all day long, or I'm just sitting here in this cramped room painting and I got paint all over me. There's nothing romantic about that, but your followers instantly assume that there's something really cool. So give them a little glimpse, glimpses into you and what your life is like, especially with relation to creating the work that you're going to release. Another thing you want to do is you want to engage your tribe. This is something that's a lot easier for artists to do than it is for me. I mean, I'm putting out a product about building business and so people don't just instantly want to just spread the word. And frankly, if you want to spread the word about PLF, that's awesome. Thank you and please do so. But in general, if you're an artist, people really feel an emotional attachment to you and to your work if you're doing everything right and they're going to want to help spread the word, especially if you give them that insider view and they got to feel like they watched the creation. They really start to feel an ownership and they're all about getting behind it. Social media is great for allowing them to spread the word. So that's how you, and you know, it's not necessarily going to be three pieces of prelaunch content, three videos, and then you release it, but you tell this story and the story is the creation. The creation of your work. So start to foreshadow the work, give them glimpses, previews, give them glimpses into the way you created it and the way that you built it. And all those things, that's the artist's path. People love that. You will build up your tribe and each release will get better and better as you build up your tribe. So the artist's path is not so much about defining that big problem and then solving it. It's more pulling people in with the story of the creation of your work.
